title: rubus tereticaulis subsp. subalpinus
intro: Rubus tereticaulis subsp. subalpinus, also known as the subalpine bramble, is a subspecies of the Rubus tereticaulis plant, a type of bramble native to mountainous regions. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its rich content of antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Rubus tereticaulis subsp. subalpinus include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
